content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 2, 1992

Diane S. Smith
3900 Crofton Way
So. San Francisco, CA 94080

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-92-429

Dear Ms. Smith:

You have requested advice on behalf of Citizens for
Responsible Government/YES on D ("Citizens") pursuant to the
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").1/ Because Citizens was formed to support a measure voted
on in the June 1992 election, your request for advice concerns
past conduct. Therefore, we treat your letter as a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

Is Citizens for Responsible Government/YES on D a controlled
committee?

CONCLUSION

Based on the information you have provided concerning the
involvement of the councilmembers, Citizens does not appear to be
a controlled committee.

_______________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Diane S. Smith
Our File No. I-92-429
Page 2

FACTS

Citizens was primarly formed to support Measure D on the
June 2, 1992 election ballot in the City of South San Francisco.
The mayor and two members of the city council participated in the
committee's activities as follows:

1.    All three signed a letter mailed by Citizens to absentee
      voters urging them to vote for Measure D.

2.    The names of all three were included in a list of
      supporters contained in another mailing sent by Citizens
      to all registered voters urging them to support Measure
      D.

3.    One councilmember's telephone number was included in a
      fundraising invitation.

4.    Two councilmembers made "sporadic and brief" appearances
      at committee meetings as interested observers.

None of the councilmembers made monetary contributions to the
committee or directed the committee's expenditures.

ANALYSIS

Section 82016 provides:

"Controlled committee" means a committee which is
controlled directly or indirectly by a candidate or
state measure proponent or which acts jointly with a
candidate, controlled committee or state measure
proponent in connection with the making of expenditures.
A candidate or state measure proponent controls a
committee if he, his agent or any other committee he
controls has a significant influence on the actions or
decisions of the committee.

The Commission has interpreted the definition of "controlled
committee" very broadly to include any significant participation
by a candidate, his or her agent, or representatives of any other
committee he or she controls in the actions of a committee.

Based on the information you have provided, it does not
appear that the councilmembers' participation in the activities of
the committee was sufficient to qualify Citizens as a controlled
committee.  Enclosed are the following advice letters issued by
the Commission which may assist you in the future:

Madden Advice Letter, No. A-85-197
Pastrick Advice Letter, No. A-87-063
Roberti Advice Letter, No. I-90-339

Diane S. Smith
Our File No. I-92-429
Page 3


     If you have additional questions concerning this letter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [Signature: Carla Wardlow]

                                        By:  Carla Wardlow
                                             Chief, Technical Assistance
                                             and Analysis Division

Enclosures
